report_name: Foxconn Hit by Nitrogen Ransomware; Gang Claims Theft of Apple, Intel Data

threat_actors.name: Nitrogen
threat_actors.description: A ransomware gang believed to be an offshoot of the Conti syndicate. Claimed responsibility for the ransomware attack against Foxconn's North American factories, alleging the theft of 8 terabytes of data.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data theft.

threat_actors.name: Conti
threat_actors.description: A notorious ransomware syndicate from which the Nitrogen gang is believed to be a splinter group, using its leaked code.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Nitrogen ransomware was used to encrypt files across Foxconn's network, causing network outages and forcing some factory operations to revert to manual processes.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The Nitrogen gang claimed to have exfiltrated 8 terabytes of data, including sensitive project files and internal documentation related to Foxconn's clients.

malware.name: Nitrogen Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload deployed by the Nitrogen gang to encrypt files on Foxconn's network and disrupt operations.

tools.name: AdFind
tools.description: Mentioned as a legitimate tool often abused by Conti-derived ransomware actors for network discovery.

tools.name: nltest
tools.description: Mentioned as a legitimate tool often abused by Conti-derived ransomware actors for domain trust discovery.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: Mentioned as a legitimate tool often abused by Conti-derived ransomware actors for remote execution.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Mentioned as a tool used by ransomware actors to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery.

identities.name: Foxconn
identities.description: The world's largest contract electronics manufacturer and victim of the Nitrogen ransomware attack, which impacted its North American operations.

identities.name: Apple
identities.description: A high-profile client of Foxconn whose sensitive project files and intellectual property were allegedly stolen in the ransomware attack.

identities.name: Intel
identities.description: A high-profile client of Foxconn whose sensitive project files and intellectual property were allegedly stolen in the ransomware attack.

identities.name: Google
identities.description: A high-profile client of Foxconn whose sensitive project files and intellectual property were allegedly stolen in the ransomware attack.

identities.name: Dell
identities.description: A client of Foxconn whose intellectual property was potentially stolen in the ransomware attack.

identities.name: Nvidia
identities.description: A client of Foxconn whose intellectual property was potentially stolen in the ransomware attack.

infrastructure.name: Nitrogen Leak Site
infrastructure.description: The dark web leak site used by the Nitrogen gang to claim responsibility for the attack and post samples of stolen data.

infrastructure.name: Nitrogen Cloud Storage
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled cloud storage accounts likely used to exfiltrate the 8 terabytes of data stolen from Foxconn.

indicators.name: Conti-derived Tool Execution (Filename)
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Conti-derived Tool Execution by Filename
id: 2b1a9c8d-4e5f-4a3b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of tools commonly abused by Conti-derived ransomware groups like Nitrogen.
references:
  - https://therecord.media/foxconn-confirms-cyberattack-north-america-factories
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1087
  - attack.t1018
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\AdFind.exe'
      - '\PsExec.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of AdFind or PsExec, tools commonly used by Conti-like ransomware groups for discovery and lateral movement.

indicators.name: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via VSSAdmin
indicators.pattern:
title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via VSSAdmin
id: 8f7e6d5c-4b3a-4e2d-8c1b-0a9f8e7d6c5b
status: experimental
description: Detects attempts to delete volume shadow copies using vssadmin.exe, a common ransomware TTP to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://therecord.media/foxconn-confirms-cyberattack-north-america-factories
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1490
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a technique used by ransomware to prevent recovery.

indicators.name: Conti-derived Tool Execution (nltest)
indicators.pattern:
title: Conti-derived Tool Execution (nltest)
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of nltest.exe, a tool that can be used for domain trust discovery by ransomware groups like Nitrogen.
references:
  - https://therecord.media/foxconn-confirms-cyberattack-north-america-factories
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1482
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\nltest.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of nltest.exe, a tool used for domain trust discovery and often abused by ransomware actors.

relationships.source_name: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via VSSAdmin
